title: gradient.c: lichen_gradient_table_init doesn't return error for NULL
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/routing/gradient.c
Lines: 106-112

In lichen_gradient_table_init():

    void lichen_gradient_table_init(struct lichen_gradient_table *table)
    {
        if (table == NULL) {
            return;  // <-- Silent failure
        }
        memset(table, 0, sizeof(*table));
    }

The function silently returns on NULL input instead of reporting the error. Compare with:
- lichen_dtn_init(): returns -EINVAL on NULL
- lichen_router_init(): returns -EINVAL on NULL

Inconsistent error handling across the codebase. Either:
1. Change return type to int and return -EINVAL on NULL
2. Use __builtin_trap() / assert for programming errors
3. Document that NULL is undefined behavior
close_reason: Updated init to return -EINVAL on NULL and changed return type to int; header updated for consistency
